questionHtml:
Which combination of ΔH1, ΔH2, and ΔH3 would give the enthalpy of the reaction?

CS2 (l) + 3O2 (g) → CO2 (g) + 2SO2 (g)

ΔH1  C (s) + O2 (g) → CO2 (g)
ΔH2  S (s) + O2 (g) → SO2 (g)
ΔH3  C (s) + 2S (s) → CS2 (l)

A.  ΔH = ΔH1 + ΔH2 + ΔH3

B.  ΔH = ΔH1 + ΔH2 − ΔH3

C.  ΔH = ΔH1 + 2(ΔH2) + ΔH3

D.  ΔH = ΔH1 + 2(ΔH2) − ΔH3

meta.title: Which combination of ΔH1, ΔH2, and ΔH3 would give the enthalpy of the reaction? CS2 (l) + 3O2 (g) → CO2 (g) + 2SO2 (g) ΔH1 C (s) + O2 (g) → CO2 (g)ΔH2 S (s) + O2 (g) → SO2 (g)ΔH3 C
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 1.2.2—Hess’s law states that the enthalpy change for a reaction is independent of the pathway between the initial and final states. Apply Hess’s law to calculate enthalpy changes in multistep reactions.
